description:
You are working on a small toolkit for a fellow script-developer who needs to merge several already-existing CSV-style text files with nothing but the standard GNU core-utils (cut, paste, sort, printf, etc.).  
  Three source files already exist inside “/home/user/data”.  
  1. /home/user/data/people.txt        – comma–separated, **exact** column order: id,first_name,last_name,department  
  2. /home/user/data/salaries.txt      – comma–separated, **exact** column order: id,salary,bonus,year  
  3. /home/user/data/addresses.txt     – comma–separated, **exact** column order: id,city,state,zip  

  The developer wants one consolidated report that contains **only** the following seven columns, in **this exact order** and with no extra spaces:  
     id,first_name,last_name,department,city,state,salary  

  Requirements you must satisfy:

  A. Create the directory “/home/user/output” if it does not already exist.  
  B. Using purely cut/paste (or their functional equivalents), build the file  
        /home/user/output/employee_report_2023.csv  
     • It must be comma-delimited, plain text, UTF-8.  
     • The first line is the header shown above.  
     • All subsequent lines are sorted numerically by id (ascending) and must preserve the exact text already present in the source files for each field (capitalisation, punctuation, etc.).  
     • Do not include bonus, zip, or any other columns.  
     • There must be **exactly** one newline (UNIX style, “\n”) at the very end of the file – no blank line afterwards.  

  C. Produce a small validation log called  
        /home/user/output/employee_report.log  
     with the following fixed three-line format (each entry on its own line, no surrounding spaces):  
        ROW_COUNT=<number_of_data_rows>  
        AVERAGE_SALARY=<floating_point_with_two_decimals>  
        GENERATED_ON=<ISO-8601_timestamp_to_seconds>  
     • The timestamp must be in local time and follow the pattern YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS (e.g. “2023-08-14T16:07:33”).  
     • Use printf/awk/bc or any other standard utilities to calculate the average salary, rounded to exactly two digits after the decimal point (banker’s rounding not required; printf “%.2f” is acceptable).  
     • “ROW_COUNT” is strictly the number of *data* lines in employee_report_2023.csv (header not included).  

  D. Make sure both output files are world-readable (chmod 644 or equivalent).  

  The automated grader will check **all** of the following:  
     1. /home/user/output/employee_report_2023.csv exists.  
     2. Header line matches exactly:  
           id,first_name,last_name,department,city,state,salary  
     3. Each data line matches its corresponding id from the source files and appears in ascending order (1, 2, 3 …).  
     4. The file has precisely 7 comma-separated fields per line and ends with a single newline.  
     5. /home/user/output/employee_report.log exists, is three lines long, and row count & average match the data inside the CSV file.  
     6. File permissions are correct (numeric 644).  

  Use only what is present in a standard Linux environment; no third-party utilities.  
  Hint: think “cut ‑d, -f”, “sort -t, -k”, “paste -d,” and temporary files in /tmp.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data the test harness will rely on.
    These files already exist before the agent starts.
  -->

  <!-- Source file #1 -->
  <file path="/home/user/data/people.txt">
1,John,Doe,Engineering
2,Jane,Smith,Marketing
3,Bob,Johnson,Sales
4,Alice,Williams,Engineering
5,Mike,Brown,Support
  </file>

  <!-- Source file #2 -->
  <file path="/home/user/data/salaries.txt">
1,90000,5000,2023
2,80000,4000,2023
3,75000,3000,2023
4,95000,6000,2023
5,60000,2000,2023
  </file>

  <!-- Source file #3 -->
  <file path="/home/user/data/addresses.txt">
1,Seattle,WA,98101
2,Portland,OR,97201
3,San Francisco,CA,94105
4,Boston,MA,02108
5,Austin,TX,73301
  </file>

  <!-- Expected final CSV -->
  <file path="/home/user/output/employee_report_2023.csv">
id,first_name,last_name,department,city,state,salary
1,John,Doe,Engineering,Seattle,WA,90000
2,Jane,Smith,Marketing,Portland,OR,80000
3,Bob,Johnson,Sales,San Francisco,CA,75000
4,Alice,Williams,Engineering,Boston,MA,95000
5,Mike,Brown,Support,Austin,TX,60000
  </file>

  <!-- Expected log file content (timestamp is wildcard) -->
  <file path="/home/user/output/employee_report.log">
ROW_COUNT=5
AVERAGE_SALARY=80000.00
GENERATED_ON=*
  </file>

  <!-- File-permission expectations -->
  <permissions path="/home/user/output/employee_report_2023.csv" mode="0644"/>
  <permissions path="/home/user/output/employee_report.log" mode="0644"/>
name: task_000000_8950465e
